title: GreenStack Sustainability Hack 2025
description: GreenStack convened 250 developers to build software solutions for environmental sustainability. Tracks covered carbon accounting, renewable energy optimization, circular economy platforms, and green Web3 incentives. Partners included the UN Environment Programme and major cleantech VCs. This closed hackathon produced 68 submissions, with 12 projects receiving seed funding follow-ups.
rules: Projects must demonstrate measurable environmental impact metrics. Open-source encouraged. Teams of 2–5. Greenwashing or unsubstantiated claims led to disqualification.
eligibility: Open to all developers passionate about climate tech. No prior sustainability experience required.

tracks.name: Carbon Intelligence
tracks.description: ML models for emissions tracking, forecasting, and reduction recommendations.
tracks.problemStatement: Build a tool that estimates Scope 3 emissions from supply chain data and recommends top 3 reduction strategies with projected impact.

tracks.name: Green DeFi & Tokenized Credits
tracks.description: Blockchain-based carbon credit marketplaces and incentive mechanisms.
tracks.problemStatement: Create a smart contract system for tokenizing verified carbon offsets with transparent provenance on-chain.

tracks.name: Sustainability DevTools
tracks.description: Developer tools for measuring and reducing software carbon footprint.
tracks.problemStatement: Build a CI plugin that estimates the carbon cost of each deployment based on cloud region, instance type, and runtime.

tracks.name: Climate NLP & Knowledge Graphs
tracks.description: AI systems for parsing climate policy documents and surfacing actionable insights.
tracks.problemStatement: Build an NLP pipeline that extracts climate commitments from corporate ESG reports and scores alignment with Paris Agreement targets.

timeline.label: Registration Closes
timeline.description: Team lock and impact metric training.

timeline.label: Judging
timeline.description: Impact and technical panels review submissions.

timeline.label: Awards & Fundraising
timeline.description: Winners announced. VC office hours for top 20 teams.

faqs.question: How was environmental impact measured?
faqs.answer: Teams used our impact calculator template with kg CO2e, water, and waste metrics.

faqs.question: Were carbon offsets provided?
faqs.answer: The event itself was carbon-neutral via Patch offsets. Projects did not need offsets.

faqs.question: Could we use proprietary company data?
faqs.answer: Only with written permission. Public datasets were recommended.

faqs.question: What happened to winning projects?
faqs.answer: 12 teams received seed funding intros. 8 projects continued as open-source.

faqs.question: Was Web3 required for the green DeFi track?
faqs.answer: Smart contracts were required for that track only. Other tracks had no blockchain requirement.

faqs.question: Will GreenStack return?
faqs.answer: GreenStack 2026 is scheduled for Earth Day week. Watch beetlex.io for announcements.
